channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ursula Andress in the 1970s: Her Transition to European Cinema and Enduring Icon Status
item.description: Ursula Andress, the Swiss actress and model born on March 19, 1936, became an international sex symbol after her iconic role as Honey Ryder in the 1962 James Bond film Dr. No. The 1970s marked a signi...

item.title: Boxer diversifies revenue streams through data analytics, media platforms and digital services
item.description: Boxer, the popular South African retailer, is expanding beyond its traditional grocery and liquor sales by developing new income streams from its large customer base. The company is investing in data ...

item.title: APC Oyo Holds Peaceful House of Reps Primaries as 74 Aspirants Contest for 2027 Tickets
item.description: Mojeed Alabi, di Chairman of di APC House of Representatives Primaries Election Committee for Oyo State, don declare say di primary exercise wey dem do across di 14 federal constituencies in di state ...

item.title: Inside the Latest Trends and Icons of Luxury Travel in 2026
item.description: Fin Magazine’s 2026 travel edition explores the evolving world of luxury travel, highlighting experiences, personalities, and innovations shaping the industry. From Ian Schrager, the legendary founder...

item.title: Sam Kerr to depart Chelsea at end of season after six successful years
item.description: Australian football star and Matildas captain Sam Kerr is set to leave Chelsea after more than six years with the London club. The 32-year-old will depart when her contract expires this summer, with h...

item.title: Veteran Pakistani journalist and intellectual Altaf Hassan Qureshi passes away at 94 in Lahore
item.description: Renowned journalist, writer, columnist and intellectual Altaf Hassan Qureshi breathed his last in Lahore on Saturday. He was 94 years old. His son Kamran Altaf confirmed the sad news and informed that...

item.title: Maldivian diver dies during recovery operation for Italian divers in Vaavu Atoll cave
item.description: A Maldivian military diver has died while searching for the bodies of four Italian divers who went missing inside an underwater cave in Vaavu Atoll, Maldives. The Italians, a group of five, were explo...

item.title: Rigathi Gachagua Returns Early from UK Trip Due to Rising Fuel Prices in Kenya
item.description: Former Deputy President Rigathi Gachagua has decided to shorten his planned one-month trip to the United Kingdom to just five or six days, citing the ongoing fuel price crisis affecting ordinary Kenya...

item.title: Glenn Close’s Transformative Role as Marquise de Merteuil in Dangerous Liaisons
item.description: On the set of the 1988 film Dangerous Liaisons, Glenn Close underwent an intense transformation to portray Marquise Isabelle de Merteuil. Filmed in authentic French châteaus, the production prioritize...

item.title: Scheduled Power Outages in Nairobi, Machakos, and Kisumu on Sunday, May 17
item.description: Kenya Power and Lighting Company (KPLC) has announced planned electricity interruptions affecting parts of Nairobi, Machakos, and Kisumu counties on Sunday, May 17. The company says the outages are ne...

item.title: Police neutralise five terrorists in Bannu following Fateh Khel checkpoint attack
item.description: In Bannu district of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistani law enforcement agencies successfully killed five terrorists during separate intelligence-based operations (IBOs). According to a Regional Police Off...

item.title: Akin Rotimi emerges winner of APC primary for Ekiti North I Federal Constituency
item.description: The spokesperson of the House of Representatives, Hon. Akin Rotimi Jr., has officially won the APC primary election for Ekiti North I Federal Constituency, securing 22 out of 24 wards in his area. The...

item.title: Milan Design Week 2026 Features Striking Inflatable Installations
item.description: Milan Design Week 2026 stood out for its creative use of inflatable structures and air-filled installations that transformed public and historic spaces across the city. The event embraced bold, playfu...

item.title: Tanzania reports significant growth in honey exports
item.description: Tanzania has recorded a notable increase in honey exports, signaling positive growth in the country's agricultural trade sector. This rise reflects stronger production capacities and better access to ...

item.title: How Young People in 2026 Experience Free Will in Everyday Life
item.description: In 2026, the concept of free will has evolved into a performative and emotional experience, especially among young people globally. Social media trends show individuals engaging in absurd yet harmless...

item.title: Celebrating Kathleen Turner’s Iconic Career in 1980s Film
item.description: Kathleen Turner emerged as one of the most influential actresses of the 1980s, captivating audiences with her unique blend of sultry charm, intelligence, and commanding presence. Known for her husky v...

item.title: Five People Killed, Ten Rescued After Abuja Construction Site Collapse
item.description: Na wah o! On Saturday for Durumi 3 near Gudu Market, Abuja, one three-storey building wey dem dey construct collapse. As e happen, five people don die while ten others dey alive after dem rescue dem f...

item.title: Faith Kipyegon wins 5,000m and Ferdinand Omanyala runs sub-10 100m in Shanghai Diamond League
item.description: Kenya’s athletics stars made strong statements at the Shanghai Diamond League on Saturday. Triple Olympic 1,500m champion Faith Kipyegon won the women’s 5,000m in a world-leading time of 14:24.14, mar...

item.title: Government training programmes struggle to reduce South Africa's unemployment rate
item.description: South Africa’s unemployment crisis continues to worsen despite substantial investment in post-school education and training. The Department of Higher Education and Training (DHET) reports using labour...

item.title: UP alumni political groups urge Senate President Cayetano to resign over mishandling of dela Rosa custody
item.description: Former members of rival student political groups from the University of the Philippines have publicly joined forces to call for the resignation of Senate President Alan Peter Cayetano. The coalition, ...
